title: Control line guidance system for downhole applications
abstract: A downhole tool including a tubular member having an outer surface and an inner surface defining a flow bore. An expandable member is arranged on the outer surface. The expandable member is selectively radially outwardly expandable. An activation mechanism is operatively connected to the expandable member. The activation mechanism is operable to radially outwardly expand the expandable member. A control line extends along the outer surface and over the expandable member. A control line guidance system is operable to shift the control line radially outwardly from the outer surface.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A downhole tool (116) comprising: a tubular (46) member including an outer surface (54) and an inner surface (56) defining a flow bore (58); an expandable member (42) arranged on the outer surface (54), the expandable member (42) being selectively radially outwardly expandable; an activation mechanism (118) operatively connected to the expandable member (42), the activation mechanism (118) being operable to radially outwardly expand the expandable member (42); a control line (50) extending along the outer surface (54) and over the expandable member (42); and a control line guidance system (124) operable to shift the control line (50) radially outwardly from the outer surface (54). 2. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 1, wherein the control line guidance system (124) is operatively connected to the activation mechanism (118). 3. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 2, wherein the control line guidance system (124) includes a linkage member having a first end (98) coupled to the control line (50) and a second end (100) pivotally connected relative to the outer surface (54). 4. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 3, further comprising: another linkage member extending from the linkage member to the activation mechanism (118). 5. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 4, wherein the activation mechanism (118) includes a setting piston (66) arranged radially outwardly of the outer surface (54), the another linkage member being connected between the setting piston (66) and the linkage member. 6. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 2, wherein the control line guidance system (124) includes a plurality of arcuate segments (144) disposed annularly about the outer surface (54) and a plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146) disposed annularly about the outer surface (54) in an alternating manner, wherein axial movement of the plurality of arcuate segments (144) relative to the plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146) shifts the control line (50) radially outwardly. 7. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 6, further comprising: an annular base portion (120) shiftably disposed on the outer surface (54), the annular base portion (120) being operable to shift the plurality of arcuate segments (144) towards the plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146). 8. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 7, wherein the control line (50) is supported by the annular base portion (120). 9. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 8, wherein the annular base portion (120) includes a groove (137), the control line (50) being disposed in the groove (137). 10. The downhole tool (116) according to claim 1, wherein the expandable member (42) comprises a packer (42). 11. A resource exploration and recovery system (10) comprising: a surface system (16); a subsurface system including a tubular (46) string extending from the surface system (16) into a wellbore (34), the tubular (46) string including a downhole tool (116) comprising: a tubular (46) member including an outer surface (54) and an inner surface (56) defining a flow bore (58); an expandable member (42) arranged on the outer surface (54), the expandable member (42) being selectively radially outwardly expandable; an activation mechanism (118) operatively connected to the expandable member (42), the activation mechanism (118) being operable to radially outwardly expand the expandable member (42); a control line (50) extending along the outer surface (54) and over the expandable member (42); and a control line guidance system (124) operable to shift the control line (50) radially outwardly from the outer surface (54). 12. The resource exploration and recovery system (10) according to claim 11, wherein the control line guidance system (124) is operatively connected to the activation mechanism (118). 13. The resource exploration and recovery system (10) according to claim 12, wherein the control line guidance system (124) includes a plurality of arcuate segments (144) disposed annularly about the outer surface (54) and a plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146) disposed annularly about the outer surface (54) in an alternating manner, wherein axial movement of the plurality of arcuate segments (144) relative to the plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146) shifts the control line (50) radially outwardly. 14. The resource exploration and recovery system (10) according to claim 13, further comprising: an annular base portion (120) shiftably disposed on the outer surface 

(54), the annular base portion (120) being operable to shift the plurality of arcuate segments (144) towards the plurality of opposing arcuate segments (146). 15. A method of shifting a control line (50) radially outwardly of a tubular (46), the method comprising: initiating an activation mechanism (118) to radially outwardly expand an expandable member (42) arranged on an outer surface (54) of a tubular (46); actuating a control line guidance system (124) with the activation mechanism (118); and radially outwardly shifting the control line (50) relative to the outer surface (54) with the control line guidance system (124).
